title: The Ultimate Guide to Beating Procrastination
author: Tklesson Team
content:
You know the feeling. An assignment is due tomorrow. You've had two weeks to work on it. Somehow, you're starting at 10 PM the night before. Again.

Procrastination isn't laziness. It's an emotional regulation problem. You're not avoiding the work — you're avoiding the discomfort that comes with it.

Here's how to break the cycle.

The 2-Minute Rule

If a task takes less than two minutes, do it immediately. Reply to that email. Put away your laundry. File that document. This builds momentum and clears mental clutter.

The 5-Minute Trick

Tell yourself you'll work on something for just five minutes. Anyone can do five minutes. The magic? Once you start, you'll usually keep going. The hardest part is beginning.

Break It Down

'Write a 10-page essay' is overwhelming. 'Write the introduction paragraph' is manageable. Break every task into ridiculously small steps. Then do one step.

Remove Friction

If you want to study in the morning, lay out your books the night before. If you want to exercise, sleep in your workout clothes. Lower the barrier to starting.

Add Friction to Distractions

Delete social media apps from your phone. Use website blockers. Put your phone in another room. Make bad habits harder to access.

The 10-10-10 Rule

Ask yourself: How will I feel about this task in 10 minutes? In 10 hours? In 10 days? Future you will thank present you for starting now.

Forgive Yourself

Guilt and shame about procrastination just fuel more procrastination. If you wasted an hour, don't waste another hour feeling bad about it. Just start now.

The best time to start was yesterday. The second best time is now.
